stories.headline: Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine
stories.summary: At least 18 killed, dozens injured and others trapped under collapsed buildings after attacks on five Ukrainian citiesVolodymyr Zelenskyy has asked Donald Trump to send Patriot missiles to Ukraine after a devastating Russian attack killed at least 18 people and injured dozens more.Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. stories.headline: ‘Trapped’: Gaza patients flown to Iraq stuck in administrative limbo
stories.summary: Palestinian patients stripped of their documents are confined inside a Baghdad medical centre.

stories.headline: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble after Russian strikes kill 22 people
stories.summary: An eight-year-old boy killed in a strike on an apartment block are among the dead, officials say.

stories.headline: Magyar teases imminent deal on Hungarian minority to lift veto on Kyiv's EU accession
stories.summary: Magyar says he is "very optimistic" about a deal on minority rights in Ukraine after years of political impasse. An agreement between Budapest and Kyiv on the delicate matter would allow for EU accession talks to begin.

stories.headline: France detains tanker linked to Iranian network accused of moving Russian oil
stories.summary: The seizure is the latest evidence of France's efforts to crack down on vessels suspected of helping Russia evade sanctions imposed following Moscow's invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Two people shot dead in Kenya protests against Ebola plan
stories.summary: Kenyan police shot and killed two people who were protesting a US-backed plan to establish an Ebola quarantine facility in central Kenya.

stories.headline: British couple lose Iran jail sentence appeal, family says
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey.

stories.headline: Two people shot dead amid Kenya protests against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
stories.summary: The US plan has sparked public anger and led to demonstrations near the site of the proposed treatment facility.

stories.headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
stories.summary: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

stories.headline: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: Trump appoints real estate heir Bill Pulte as national intelligence chief
stories.summary: Housing industry executive and staunch ally of the president to take up role in acting capacity

stories.headline: Perplexity Is 'Chip Agnostic,' Says CEO
stories.summary: Computex in Taiwan is putting the spotlight on the companies building the hardware behind the AI boom. One of those companies is Perplexity, which took the stage with Intel to unveil the world's first "hybrid local/server agentic inference orchestrator." Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Billionaire Kazakh Brokerage CEO Got SEC Enforcement Warning
stories.summary: The billionaire head of Kazakhstan-based Freedom Holding Corp. has received a so-called Wells Notice from the US Securities and Exchange Commission, a formal warning that the Wall Street regulator may take enforcement action.

stories.headline: Trump Names Bill Pulte as Acting Director of National Intelligence
stories.summary: Bill Pulte, a Trump loyalist who leads the federal housing finance agency, will oversee the U.S. intelligence agencies. He has no known background in intelligence.

stories.headline: Rubio to Testify Before Senate, Arguing for Budget Amid Iran War and Pressure on Cuba
stories.summary: The secretary of state has played a key role in the Iran deliberations, as well as in U.S. policy toward Cuba.

stories.headline: California Primary Elections: What to Watch in the Governor’s Race, L.A. Mayoral Race and More
stories.summary: The race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom has been unpredictable for months, while Mayor Karen Bass of Los Angeles is trying to fend off two challengers.

stories.headline: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Iowa, Montana and Beyond
stories.summary: Democrats have a competitive Senate primary race in Iowa, where the party is hoping to make gains, while a smokejumper is running in a contested House primary in Montana.

stories.headline: Trump Backs Off Plan for $1.8 Billion Fund That Drew Political Backlash
stories.summary: The fund has drawn backlash from critics who said it was a scheme to reward President Trump’s political allies with public benefits.

stories.headline: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa

stories.headline: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty
stories.summary: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of TuesdayDenmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition.Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Gree

stories.headline: Canada formally requests renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.headline: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
stories.summary: The deaths come after a day of violent protests against illegal migration in the town of Mossel Bay.

stories.headline: Mysteriously missing New Jersey congressman receives Trump endorsement
stories.summary: Republican Congressman Tom Kean Jr has not been seen for months but is expected to win Tuesday's election.

stories.summary: Passed by MPs, the bill proposes prison sentences for people identifying as part of the LGBTQ+ community.

stories.headline: Australian ex-minister launches crowd-funded inquiry into Aukus submarine deal
stories.summary: Inquiry will look at whether Australia will receive the submarines and whether it will make the country safer.

stories.headline: China goes after 'ghost kitchens' to rein in cut-throat food delivery apps
stories.summary: The thousands of "ghost kitchens" - online shops that don't actually exist - have spooked Chinese consumers.

stories.headline: Why Myanmar president's India visit is being closely watched
stories.summary: Myanmar is seeking to broaden diplomatic engagement after years of international criticism and isolation.

stories.headline: US Stocks Gain as Traders Evaluate Middle East, Jobs Data
stories.summary: US stocks were slightly higher on Tuesday as traders parsed the latest developments in the Middle East and fresh employment data.

stories.headline: AI Fuels $280 Billion Cybersecurity Rally as Earnings Test Looms
stories.summary: Investors are about to get a read on the durability of the soaring rally in cybersecurity stocks when two of the industry’s major players report earnings in the coming days.

stories.headline: Alphabet's $80 Billion AI Power Move | Open Interest 6/2/2026
stories.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Alphabet is raising $80 billion in equity, including an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway to help fund its AI spending plans.  Anthropic’s IPO filing raises the stakes against OpenAI. Plus, US job openings jumped in April to the highest level in almost two years.  SpaceX negotiates to pay razor-thin fees to Wall Street firms handling its IP

stories.headline: Canadian Stocks Index Hits 35,000 Amid Rally in Energy
stories.summary: The Canadian equities benchmark passed 35,000 for the first time on a broadening rally in energy and financials.

stories.headline: First Brands Had '5 Red Flags,' Says Michael Gatto
stories.summary:
The US government has joined the line of creditors impacted by alleged fraud at bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands. 
Columbia Business School Professor and Partner at Silver Point Capital, Michael Gatto discusses his take on the First Brands drama, as well as advice for young professionals in credit and distressed markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Venezuela Wants Oil Firms to Supply Their Own Power for Projects
stories.summary: Energy companies heading to Venezuela are being told to bring their own power plants to run their oil and natural gas operations and shield them from frequent blackouts on the nation’s ill-maintained electricity grid.

stories.headline: Emerging-Market Currencies Gain as Risk Appetite Rebounds
stories.summary: Most emerging market currencies advanced as hopes of a deal in the Middle East boosted risk sentiment and US data showed a strong labor market.

stories.headline: HPE Rises on Sales Outlook; Victoria's Secret Soars on Profit Beat | Stock Movers
stories.summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Victoria's Secret (VSXY) shares are soaring after the lingerie maker, now trading under symbol VSXY, reports profit metrics and net sales for its first quarter that topped expectations, including adjusted operating income that was nearly twice the consensus estimate. Management boosted its annual forecasts.

stories.headline: China opens its markets to African exports. Who benefits?
stories.summary: China's tariff-free access for many African products has renewed hopes of industrial growth. But trade remains uneven.

stories.headline: Intisar Shanib becomes first woman to head football club in Libya
stories.summary: Shanib has been appointed as the president of Darnes Sports Club, a prominent football club in Libya's Derna city.

stories.headline: Is the Taliban-Russia MoU good for Afghanistan?

stories.summary: Hundreds have rallied in Albania against plans by a Jared Kushner-linked investment firm to develop Sazan Island.

stories.headline: ‘Encroached territory’: Why India-Nepal border dispute is heating up again
stories.summary: Balendra Shah's recent comments about Nepal encroaching on Indian territory have revived a 200-year-old border dispute.

stories.summary: The Commission will criticise Italy’s lack of robust industrial plans and calls for measures to incentivise investment in capital markets as well as reform of tax system, in country-specific recommendations to be published tomorrow.

stories.headline: Cryptocurrency addiction is real — and it looks just like gambling
stories.summary: Jamie Giles is a therapist at Castle Craig, one of Scotland's leading addiction treatment centres. He spoke to Euronews about how to spot problematic crypto investing, who is most at risk and what to do about it.

stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.summary: Analysts told BBC Verify that Tehran's strikes were more extensive than publicly acknowledged.

stories.headline: Tech Stocks Drive S&P 500 to Nine-Day Winning Run: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A revival of the artificial-intelligence trade kept driving Wall Street momentum, with stocks also rising on hopes for an agreement that would end the war that has roiled financial markets around the world.

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
stories.summary: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40CA powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. stories.headline: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.headline: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
stories.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

stories.headline: Oil Swings Higher as Traders Weigh Prospects of US-Iran Deal

stories.headline: How Putin became master of the image
stories.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

stories.headline: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
stories.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes

stories.headline: Learning from Ukraine war, Hezbollah is now using fibre-optic drones to hit Israel
stories.summary: Fibre-optic drones are now Hezbollah's primary weapon against Israel's soldiers and civilians.
